verses.sanskrit: सूत उवाचमहीपतिस्त्वथ तत्कर्म गर्ह्यंविचिन्तयन्नात्मकृतं सुदुर्मना: ।अहो मया नीचमनार्यवत्कृतंनिरागसि ब्रह्मणि गूढतेजसि ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: sūta uvācamahī-patis tv atha tat-karma garhyaṁvicintayann ātma-kṛtaṁ sudurmanāḥaho mayā nīcam anārya-vat kṛtaṁnirāgasi brahmaṇi gūḍha-tejasi
verses.text: Śrī Sūta Gosvāmī said: While returning home, the King [Mahārāja Parīkṣit] felt that the act he had committed against the faultless and powerful brāhmaṇa was heinous and uncivilized. Consequently he was distressed.

verses.sanskrit: ध्रुवं ततो मे कृतदेवहेलनाद्दुरत्ययं व्यसनं नातिदीर्घात् ।तदस्तु कामं ह्यघनिष्कृताय मेयथा न कुर्यां पुनरेवमद्धा ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhruvaṁ tato me kṛta-deva-helanādduratyayaṁ vyasanaṁ nāti-dīrghāttad astu kāmaṁ hy agha-niṣkṛtāya meyathā na kuryāṁ punar evam addhā
verses.text: [King Parīkṣit thought:] Due to my neglecting the injunctions of the Supreme Lord I must certainly expect some difficulty to overcome me in the near future. I now desire without reservation that the calamity come now, for in this way I may be freed of the sinful action and not commit such an offense again.

verses.sanskrit: अद्यैव राज्यं बलमृद्धकोशंप्रकोपितब्रह्मकुलानलो मे ।दहत्वभद्रस्य पुनर्न मेऽभूत्पापीयसी धीर्द्विजदेवगोभ्य: ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: adyaiva rājyaṁ balam ṛddha-kośaṁprakopita-brahma-kulānalo medahatv abhadrasya punar na me ’bhūtpāpīyasī dhīr dvija-deva-gobhyaḥ
verses.text: I am uncivilized and sinful due to my neglect of brahminical culture, God consciousness and cow protection. Therefore I wish that my kingdom, strength and riches burn up immediately by the fire of the brāhmaṇa’s wrath so that in the future I may not be guided by such inauspicious attitudes.

verses.sanskrit: अद्यैव राज्यं बलमृद्धकोशंप्रकोपितब्रह्मकुलानलो मे ।दहत्वभद्रस्य पुनर्न मेऽभूत्पापीयसी धीर्द्विजदेवगोभ्य: ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: adyaiva rājyaṁ balam ṛddha-kośaṁprakopita-brahma-kulānalo medahatv abhadrasya punar na me ’bhūtpāpīyasī dhīr dvija-deva-gobhyaḥ
verses.text: I am uncivilized and sinful due to my neglect of brahminical culture, God consciousness and cow protection. Therefore I wish that my kingdom, strength and riches burn up immediately by the fire of the brāhmaṇa’s wrath so that in the future I may not be guided by such inauspicious attitudes.

verses.sanskrit: स चिन्तयन्नित्थमथाश‍ृणोद् यथामुने: सुतोक्तो निऋर्तिस्तक्षकाख्य: ।स साधु मेने न चिरेण तक्षका-नलं प्रसक्तस्य विरक्तिकारणम् ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa cintayann ittham athāśṛṇod yathāmuneḥ sutokto nirṛtis takṣakākhyaḥsa sādhu mene na cireṇa takṣakā-nalaṁ prasaktasya virakti-kāraṇam
verses.text: While the King was thus repenting, he received news of his imminent death, which would be due to the bite of a snake-bird, occasioned by the curse spoken by the sage’s son. The King accepted this as good news, for it would be the cause of his indifference toward worldly things.

verses.sanskrit: स चिन्तयन्नित्थमथाश‍ृणोद् यथामुने: सुतोक्तो निऋर्तिस्तक्षकाख्य: ।स साधु मेने न चिरेण तक्षका-नलं प्रसक्तस्य विरक्तिकारणम् ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa cintayann ittham athāśṛṇod yathāmuneḥ sutokto nirṛtis takṣakākhyaḥsa sādhu mene na cireṇa takṣakā-nalaṁ prasaktasya virakti-kāraṇam
verses.text: While the King was thus repenting, he received news of his imminent death, which would be due to the bite of a snake-bird, occasioned by the curse spoken by the sage’s son. The King accepted this as good news, for it would be the cause of his indifference toward worldly things.

verses.sanskrit: अथो विहायेमममुं च लोकंविमर्शितौ हेयतया पुरस्तात् ।कृष्णाङ्‌घ्रिसेवामधिमन्यमानउपाविशत् प्रायममर्त्यनद्याम् ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: atho vihāyemam amuṁ ca lokaṁvimarśitau heyatayā purastātkṛṣṇāṅghri-sevām adhimanyamānaupāviśat prāyam amartya-nadyām
verses.text: Mahārāja Parīkṣit sat down firmly on the banks of the Ganges to concentrate his mind in Kṛṣṇa consciousness, rejecting all other practices of self-realization, because transcendental loving service to Kṛṣṇa is the greatest achievement, superseding all other methods.

verses.sanskrit: या वै लसच्छ्रीतुलसीविमिश्र-कृष्णाङ्‌घ्रिरेण्वभ्यधिकाम्बुनेत्री ।पुनाति लोकानुभयत्र सेशान्कस्तां न सेवेत मरिष्यमाण: ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: yā vai lasac-chrī-tulasī-vimiśra-kṛṣṇāṅghri-reṇv-abhyadhikāmbu-netrīpunāti lokān ubhayatra seśānkas tāṁ na seveta mariṣyamāṇaḥ
verses.text: The river [by which the King sat to fast] carries the most auspicious water, which is mixed with the dust of the lotus feet of the Lord and tulasī leaves. Therefore that water sanctifies the three worlds inside and outside and even sanctifies Lord Śiva and other demigods. Consequently everyone who is destined to die must take shelter of this river.

verses.sanskrit: इति व्यवच्छिद्य स पाण्डवेय:प्रायोपवेशं प्रति विष्णुपद्याम् ।दधौ मुकुन्दाङ्‌घ्रिमनन्यभावोमुनिव्रतो मुक्तसमस्तसङ्ग: ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: iti vyavacchidya sa pāṇḍaveyaḥprāyopaveśaṁ prati viṣṇu-padyāmdadhau mukundāṅghrim ananya-bhāvomuni-vrato mukta-samasta-saṅgaḥ
verses.text: Thus the King, the worthy descendant of the Pāṇḍavas, decided once and for all and sat on the Ganges’ bank to fast until death and give himself up to the lotus feet of Lord Kṛṣṇa, who alone is able to award liberation. So, freeing himself from all kinds of associations and attachments, he accepted the vows of a sage.

verses.sanskrit: तत्रोपजग्मुर्भुवनं पुनानामहानुभावा मुनय: सशिष्या: ।प्रायेणतीर्थाभिगमापदेशै:स्वयं हि तीर्थानि पुनन्ति सन्त: ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: tatropajagmur bhuvanaṁ punānāmahānubhāvā munayaḥ sa-śiṣyāḥprāyeṇa tīrthābhigamāpadeśaiḥsvayaṁ hi tīrthāni punanti santaḥ
verses.text: At that time all the great minds and thinkers, accompanied by their disciples, and sages who could verily sanctify a place of pilgrimage just by their presence, arrived there on the plea of making a pilgrim’s journey.

verses.sanskrit: अत्रिर्वसिष्ठश्‍च्यवन: शरद्वा-नरिष्टनेमिर्भृगुरङ्गिराश्च ।पराशरो गाधिसुतोऽथ रामउतथ्य इन्द्रप्रमदेध्मवाहौ ॥ ९ ॥मेधातिथिर्देवल आर्ष्टिषेणोभारद्वाजो गौतम: पिप्पलाद: ।मैत्रेय और्व: कवष: कुम्भयोनि-र्द्वैपायनो भगवान्नारदश्च ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: atrir vasiṣṭhaś cyavanaḥ śaradvānariṣṭanemir bhṛgur aṅgirāś caparāśaro gādhi-suto ’tha rāmautathya indrapramadedhmavāhau
verses.text: From different parts of the universe there arrived great sages like Atri, Cyavana, Śaradvān, Ariṣṭanemi, Bhṛgu, Vasiṣṭha, Parāśara, Viśvāmitra, Aṅgirā, Paraśurāma, Utathya, Indrapramada, Idhmavāhu, Medhātithi, Devala, Ārṣṭiṣeṇa, Bhāradvāja, Gautama, Pippalāda, Maitreya, Aurva, Kavaṣa, Kumbhayoni, Dvaipāyana and the great personality Nārada.

verses.sanskrit: अत्रिर्वसिष्ठश्‍च्यवन: शरद्वा-नरिष्टनेमिर्भृगुरङ्गिराश्च ।पराशरो गाधिसुतोऽथ रामउतथ्य इन्द्रप्रमदेध्मवाहौ ॥ ९ ॥मेधातिथिर्देवल आर्ष्टिषेणोभारद्वाजो गौतम: पिप्पलाद: ।मैत्रेय और्व: कवष: कुम्भयोनि-र्द्वैपायनो भगवान्नारदश्च ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: atrir vasiṣṭhaś cyavanaḥ śaradvānariṣṭanemir bhṛgur aṅgirāś caparāśaro gādhi-suto ’tha rāmautathya indrapramadedhmavāhau
verses.text: From different parts of the universe there arrived great sages like Atri, Cyavana, Śaradvān, Ariṣṭanemi, Bhṛgu, Vasiṣṭha, Parāśara, Viśvāmitra, Aṅgirā, Paraśurāma, Utathya, Indrapramada, Idhmavāhu, Medhātithi, Devala, Ārṣṭiṣeṇa, Bhāradvāja, Gautama, Pippalāda, Maitreya, Aurva, Kavaṣa, Kumbhayoni, Dvaipāyana and the great personality Nārada.

verses.sanskrit: अत्रिर्वसिष्ठश्‍च्यवन: शरद्वा-नरिष्टनेमिर्भृगुरङ्गिराश्च ।पराशरो गाधिसुतोऽथ रामउतथ्य इन्द्रप्रमदेध्मवाहौ ॥ ९ ॥मेधातिथिर्देवल आर्ष्टिषेणोभारद्वाजो गौतम: पिप्पलाद: ।मैत्रेय और्व: कवष: कुम्भयोनि-र्द्वैपायनो भगवान्नारदश्च ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: atrir vasiṣṭhaś cyavanaḥ śaradvānariṣṭanemir bhṛgur aṅgirāś caparāśaro gādhi-suto ’tha rāmautathya indrapramadedhmavāhau
verses.text: From different parts of the universe there arrived great sages like Atri, Cyavana, Śaradvān, Ariṣṭanemi, Bhṛgu, Vasiṣṭha, Parāśara, Viśvāmitra, Aṅgirā, Paraśurāma, Utathya, Indrapramada, Idhmavāhu, Medhātithi, Devala, Ārṣṭiṣeṇa, Bhāradvāja, Gautama, Pippalāda, Maitreya, Aurva, Kavaṣa, Kumbhayoni, Dvaipāyana and the great personality Nārada.

verses.sanskrit: अत्रिर्वसिष्ठश्‍च्यवन: शरद्वा-नरिष्टनेमिर्भृगुरङ्गिराश्च ।पराशरो गाधिसुतोऽथ रामउतथ्य इन्द्रप्रमदेध्मवाहौ ॥ ९ ॥मेधातिथिर्देवल आर्ष्टिषेणोभारद्वाजो गौतम: पिप्पलाद: ।मैत्रेय और्व: कवष: कुम्भयोनि-र्द्वैपायनो भगवान्नारदश्च ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: atrir vasiṣṭhaś cyavanaḥ śaradvānariṣṭanemir bhṛgur aṅgirāś caparāśaro gādhi-suto ’tha rāmautathya indrapramadedhmavāhau
verses.text: From different parts of the universe there arrived great sages like Atri, Cyavana, Śaradvān, Ariṣṭanemi, Bhṛgu, Vasiṣṭha, Parāśara, Viśvāmitra, Aṅgirā, Paraśurāma, Utathya, Indrapramada, Idhmavāhu, Medhātithi, Devala, Ārṣṭiṣeṇa, Bhāradvāja, Gautama, Pippalāda, Maitreya, Aurva, Kavaṣa, Kumbhayoni, Dvaipāyana and the great personality Nārada.

verses.sanskrit: अत्रिर्वसिष्ठश्‍च्यवन: शरद्वा-नरिष्टनेमिर्भृगुरङ्गिराश्च ।पराशरो गाधिसुतोऽथ रामउतथ्य इन्द्रप्रमदेध्मवाहौ ॥ ९ ॥मेधातिथिर्देवल आर्ष्टिषेणोभारद्वाजो गौतम: पिप्पलाद: ।मैत्रेय और्व: कवष: कुम्भयोनि-र्द्वैपायनो भगवान्नारदश्च ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: atrir vasiṣṭhaś cyavanaḥ śaradvānariṣṭanemir bhṛgur aṅgirāś caparāśaro gādhi-suto ’tha rāmautathya indrapramadedhmavāhau
verses.text: From different parts of the universe there arrived great sages like Atri, Cyavana, Śaradvān, Ariṣṭanemi, Bhṛgu, Vasiṣṭha, Parāśara, Viśvāmitra, Aṅgirā, Paraśurāma, Utathya, Indrapramada, Idhmavāhu, Medhātithi, Devala, Ārṣṭiṣeṇa, Bhāradvāja, Gautama, Pippalāda, Maitreya, Aurva, Kavaṣa, Kumbhayoni, Dvaipāyana and the great personality Nārada.

verses.sanskrit: अत्रिर्वसिष्ठश्‍च्यवन: शरद्वा-नरिष्टनेमिर्भृगुरङ्गिराश्च ।पराशरो गाधिसुतोऽथ रामउतथ्य इन्द्रप्रमदेध्मवाहौ ॥ ९ ॥मेधातिथिर्देवल आर्ष्टिषेणोभारद्वाजो गौतम: पिप्पलाद: ।मैत्रेय और्व: कवष: कुम्भयोनि-र्द्वैपायनो भगवान्नारदश्च ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: atrir vasiṣṭhaś cyavanaḥ śaradvānariṣṭanemir bhṛgur aṅgirāś caparāśaro gādhi-suto ’tha rāmautathya indrapramadedhmavāhau
verses.text: From different parts of the universe there arrived great sages like Atri, Cyavana, Śaradvān, Ariṣṭanemi, Bhṛgu, Vasiṣṭha, Parāśara, Viśvāmitra, Aṅgirā, Paraśurāma, Utathya, Indrapramada, Idhmavāhu, Medhātithi, Devala, Ārṣṭiṣeṇa, Bhāradvāja, Gautama, Pippalāda, Maitreya, Aurva, Kavaṣa, Kumbhayoni, Dvaipāyana and the great personality Nārada.

verses.sanskrit: अन्ये च देवर्षिब्रह्मर्षिवर्याराजर्षिवर्या अरुणादयश्च ।नानार्षेयप्रवरान् समेता-नभ्यर्च्य राजा शिरसा ववन्दे ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: anye ca devarṣi-brahmarṣi-varyārājarṣi-varyā aruṇādayaś canānārṣeya-pravarān sametānabhyarcya rājā śirasā vavande
verses.text: There were also many other saintly demigods, kings and special royal orders called aruṇādayas [a special rank of rājarṣis] from different dynasties of sages. When they all assembled together to meet the Emperor [Parīkṣit], he received them properly and bowed his head to the ground.

verses.sanskrit: सुखोपविष्टेष्वथ तेषु भूय:कृतप्रणाम: स्वचिकीर्षितं यत् ।विज्ञापयामासविविक्तचेताउपस्थितोऽग्रेऽभिगृहीतपाणि: ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: sukhopaviṣṭeṣv atha teṣu bhūyaḥkṛta-praṇāmaḥ sva-cikīrṣitaṁ yatvijñāpayām āsa vivikta-cetāupasthito ’gre ’bhigṛhīta-pāṇiḥ
verses.text: After all the ṛṣis and others had seated themselves comfortably, the King, humbly standing before them with folded hands, told them of his decision to fast until death.

verses.sanskrit: राजोवाच
verses.transliteration: rājovācaaho vayaṁ dhanyatamā nṛpāṇāṁmahattamānugrahaṇīya-śīlāḥrājñāṁ kulaṁ brāhmaṇa-pāda-śaucāddūrād visṛṣṭaṁ bata garhya-karma
verses.text: The fortunate King said: Indeed, we are the most grateful of all the kings who are trained to get favors from the great souls. Generally you [sages] consider royalty as refuse to be rejected and left in a distant place.

verses.sanskrit: तस्यैव मेऽघस्य परावरेशोव्यासक्तचित्तस्य गृहेष्वभीक्ष्णम् ।निर्वेदमूलो द्विजशापरूपोयत्र प्रसक्तो भयमाशु धत्ते ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasyaiva me ’ghasya parāvareśovyāsakta-cittasya gṛheṣv abhīkṣṇamnirveda-mūlo dvija-śāpa-rūpoyatra prasakto bhayam āśu dhatte
verses.text: The Supreme Personality of Godhead, the controller of both the transcendental and mundane worlds, has graciously overtaken me in the form of a brāhmaṇa’s curse. Due to my being too much attached to family life, the Lord, in order to save me, has appeared before me in such a way that only out of fear I will detach myself from the world.

verses.sanskrit: तं मोपयातं प्रतियन्तु विप्रागङ्गा च देवी धृतचित्तमीशे ।द्विजोपसृष्ट: कुहकस्तक्षको वादशत्वलं गायत विष्णुगाथा: ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: taṁ mopayātaṁ pratiyantu viprāgaṅgā ca devī dhṛta-cittam īśedvijopasṛṣṭaḥ kuhakas takṣako vādaśatv alaṁ gāyata viṣṇu-gāthāḥ
verses.text: O brāhmaṇas, just accept me as a completely surrendered soul, and let mother Ganges, the representative of the Lord, also accept me in that way, for I have already taken the lotus feet of the Lord into my heart. Let the snake-bird — or whatever magical thing the brāhmaṇa created — bite me at once. I only desire that you all continue singing the deeds of Lord Viṣṇu.

verses.sanskrit: पुनश्च भूयाद्भगवत्यनन्तेरति: प्रसङ्गश्च तदाश्रयेषु ।महत्सु यां यामुपयामि सृष्टिंमैत्र्यस्तु सर्वत्र नमो द्विजेभ्य: ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: punaś ca bhūyād bhagavaty ananteratiḥ prasaṅgaś ca tad-āśrayeṣumahatsu yāṁ yām upayāmi sṛṣṭiṁmaitry astu sarvatra namo dvijebhyaḥ
verses.text: Again, offering obeisances unto all you brāhmaṇas, I pray that if I should again take my birth in the material world I will have complete attachment to the unlimited Lord Kṛṣṇa, association with His devotees and friendly relations with all living beings.

verses.sanskrit: इति स्म राजाध्यवसाययुक्त:प्राचीनमूलेषु कुशेषु धीर: ।उदङ्‍मुखोदक्षिणकूल आस्तेसमुद्रपत्‍न्‍या: स्वसुतन्यस्तभार: ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: iti sma rājādhyavasāya-yuktaḥprācīna-mūleṣu kuśeṣu dhīraḥudaṅ-mukho dakṣiṇa-kūla āstesamudra-patnyāḥ sva-suta-nyasta-bhāraḥ
verses.text: In perfect self-control, Mahārāja Parīkṣit sat down on a seat of straw, with straw-roots facing the east, placed on the southern bank of the Ganges, and he himself faced the north. Just previously he had given charge of his kingdom over to his son.

verses.sanskrit: एवं च तस्मिन्नरदेवदेवेप्रायोपविष्टे दिवि देवसङ्घा: ।प्रशस्यभूमौ व्यकिरन् प्रसूनै-र्मुदा मुहुर्दुन्दुभयश्च नेदु: ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁ ca tasmin nara-deva-deveprāyopaviṣṭe divi deva-saṅghāḥpraśasya bhūmau vyakiran prasūnairmudā muhur dundubhayaś ca neduḥ
verses.text: Thus the King, Mahārāja Parīkṣit, sat to fast until death. All the demigods of the higher planets praised the King’s actions and in pleasure continually scattered flowers over the earth and beat celestial drums.

verses.sanskrit: महर्षयो वै समुपागता येप्रशस्य साध्वित्यनुमोदमाना: ।ऊचु: प्रजानुग्रहशीलसारायदुत्तमश्लोकगुणाभिरूपम् ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: maharṣayo vai samupāgatā yepraśasya sādhv ity anumodamānāḥūcuḥ prajānugraha-śīla-sārāyad uttama-śloka-guṇābhirūpam
verses.text: All the great sages who were assembled there also praised the decision of Mahārāja Parīkṣit, and they expressed their approval by saying “Very good.” Naturally the sages are inclined to do good to common men, for they have all the qualitative powers of the Supreme Lord. Therefore they were very much pleased to see Mahārāja Parīkṣit, a devotee of the Lord, and they spoke as follows.

verses.sanskrit: न वा इदं राजर्षिवर्य चित्रंभवत्सु कृष्णं समनुव्रतेषु ।येऽध्यासनं राजकिरीटजुष्टंसद्यो जहुर्भगवत्पार्श्वकामा: ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: na vā idaṁ rājarṣi-varya citraṁbhavatsu kṛṣṇaṁ samanuvrateṣuye ’dhyāsanaṁ rāja-kirīṭa-juṣṭaṁsadyo jahur bhagavat-pārśva-kāmāḥ
verses.text: [The sages said:] O chief of all the saintly kings of the Pāṇḍu dynasty who are strictly in the line of Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa! It is not at all astonishing that you give up your throne, which is decorated with the helmets of many kings, to achieve eternal association with the Personality of Godhead.

verses.sanskrit: न वा इदं राजर्षिवर्य चित्रंभवत्सु कृष्णं समनुव्रतेषु ।येऽध्यासनं राजकिरीटजुष्टंसद्यो जहुर्भगवत्पार्श्वकामा: ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: na vā idaṁ rājarṣi-varya citraṁbhavatsu kṛṣṇaṁ samanuvrateṣuye ’dhyāsanaṁ rāja-kirīṭa-juṣṭaṁsadyo jahur bhagavat-pārśva-kāmāḥ
verses.text: [The sages said:] O chief of all the saintly kings of the Pāṇḍu dynasty who are strictly in the line of Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa! It is not at all astonishing that you give up your throne, which is decorated with the helmets of many kings, to achieve eternal association with the Personality of Godhead.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वे वयं तावदिहास्महेऽथकलेवरं यावदसौ विहाय ।लोकं परं विरजस्कं विशोकंयास्यत्ययं भागवतप्रधान: ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: sarve vayaṁ tāvad ihāsmahe ’thakalevaraṁ yāvad asau vihāyalokaṁ paraṁ virajaskaṁ viśokaṁyāsyaty ayaṁ bhāgavata-pradhānaḥ
verses.text: We shall all wait here until the foremost devotee of the Lord, Mahārāja Parīkṣit, returns to the supreme planet, which is completely free from all mundane contamination and all kinds of lamentation.

verses.sanskrit: समागता: सर्वत एव सर्वेवेदा यथा मूर्तिधरास्त्रिपृष्ठे ।नेहाथ नामुत्र च कश्चनार्थऋते परानुग्रहमात्मशीलम् ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: samāgatāḥ sarvata eva sarvevedā yathā mūrti-dharās tri-pṛṣṭhenehātha nāmutra ca kaścanārthaṛte parānugraham ātma-śīlam
verses.text: The King said: O great sages, you have all very kindly assembled here, having come from all parts of the universe. You are all as good as supreme knowledge personified, who resides in the planet above the three worlds [Satyaloka]. Consequently you are naturally inclined to do good to others, and but for this you have no interest, either in this life or in the next.

verses.sanskrit: ततश्च व: पृच्छ्‍यमिमं विपृच्छेविश्रभ्य विप्रा इति कृत्यतायाम् ।सर्वात्मना म्रियमाणैश्च कृत्यंशुद्धं च तत्रामृशताभियुक्ता: ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: tataś ca vaḥ pṛcchyam imaṁ vipṛccheviśrabhya viprā iti kṛtyatāyāmsarvātmanā mriyamāṇaiś ca kṛtyaṁśuddhaṁ ca tatrāmṛśatābhiyuktāḥ
verses.text: O trustworthy brāhmaṇas, I now ask you about my immediate duty. Please, after proper deliberation, tell me of the unalloyed duty of everyone in all circumstances, and specifically of those who are just about to die.

verses.sanskrit: तत्राभवद्भगवान् व्यासपुत्रोयद‍ृच्छया गामटमानोऽनपेक्ष: ।अलक्ष्यलिङ्गो निजलाभतुष्टोवृतश्च बालैरवधूतवेष: ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: tatrābhavad bhagavān vyāsa-putroyadṛcchayā gām aṭamāno ’napekṣaḥalakṣya-liṅgo nija-lābha-tuṣṭovṛtaś ca bālair avadhūta-veṣaḥ
verses.text: At that moment there appeared the powerful son of Vyāsadeva, who traveled over the earth disinterested and satisfied with himself. He did not manifest any symptoms of belonging to any social order or status of life. He was surrounded with women and children, and he dressed as if others had neglected him.

verses.sanskrit: तं द्व‌्यष्टवर्षंसुकुमारपाद-करोरुबाह्वंसकपोलगात्रम् ।चार्वायताक्षोन्नसतुल्यकर्ण-सुभ्र्वाननंकम्बुसुजातकण्ठम् ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: taṁ dvyaṣṭa-varṣaṁ su-kumāra-pāda-karoru-bāhv-aṁsa-kapola-gātramcārv-āyatākṣonnasa-tulya-karṇa-subhrv-ānanaṁ kambu-sujāta-kaṇṭham
verses.text: This son of Vyāsadeva was only sixteen years old. His legs, hands, thighs, arms, shoulders, forehead and the other parts of his body were all delicately formed. His eyes were beautifully wide, and his nose and ears were highly raised. He had a very attractive face, and his neck was well formed and beautiful like a conchshell.

verses.sanskrit: निगूढजत्रुं पृथुतुङ्गवक्षस-मावर्तनाभिं वलिवल्गूदरं च ।दिगम्बरं वक्त्रविकीर्णकेशंप्रलम्बबाहुं स्वमरोत्तमाभम् ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: nigūḍha-jatruṁ pṛthu-tuṅga-vakṣasamāvarta-nābhiṁ vali-valgūdaraṁ cadig-ambaraṁ vaktra-vikīrṇa-keśaṁpralamba-bāhuṁ svamarottamābham
verses.text: His collarbone was fleshy, his chest broad and thick, his navel deep and his abdomen beautifully striped. His arms were long, and curly hair was strewn over his beautiful face. He was naked, and the hue of his body reflected that of Lord Kṛṣṇa.

verses.sanskrit: स विष्णुरातोऽतिथय आगतायतस्मै सपर्यां शिरसाजहार ।ततो निवृत्ता ह्यबुधा: स्त्रियोऽर्भकामहासने सोपविवेश पूजित: ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa viṣṇu-rāto ’tithaya āgatāyatasmai saparyāṁ śirasājahāratato nivṛttā hy abudhāḥ striyo ’rbhakāmahāsane sopaviveśa pūjitaḥ
verses.text: Mahārāja Parīkṣit, who is also known as Viṣṇurāta [one who is always protected by Viṣṇu], bowed his head to receive the chief guest, Śukadeva Gosvāmī. At that time all the ignorant women and boys ceased following Śrīla Śukadeva. Receiving respect from all, Śukadeva Gosvāmī took his exalted seat.

verses.sanskrit: स संवृतस्तत्र महान् महीयसांब्रह्मर्षिराजर्षिदेवर्षिसङ्घै: ।व्यरोचतालं भगवान् यथेन्दु-र्ग्रहर्क्षतारानिकरै: परीत: ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: sa saṁvṛtas tatra mahān mahīyasāṁbrahmarṣi-rājarṣi-devarṣi-saṅghaiḥvyarocatālaṁ bhagavān yathendurgraharkṣa-tārā-nikaraiḥ parītaḥ
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī was then surrounded by saintly sages and demigods just as the moon is surrounded by stars, planets and other heavenly bodies. His presence was gorgeous, and he was respected by all.

verses.sanskrit: प्रशान्तमासीनमकुण्ठमेधसंमुनिं नृपो भागवतोऽभ्युपेत्य ।प्रणम्य मूर्ध्नावहित: कृताञ्जलि-र्नत्वा गिरा सूनृतयान्वपृच्छत् ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: praśāntam āsīnam akuṇṭha-medhasaṁmuniṁ nṛpo bhāgavato ’bhyupetyapraṇamya mūrdhnāvahitaḥ kṛtāñjalirnatvā girā sūnṛtayānvapṛcchat
verses.text: The sage Śrī Śukadeva Gosvāmī sat perfectly pacified, intelligent and ready to answer any question without hesitation. The great devotee, Mahārāja Parīkṣit, approached him, offered his respects by bowing before him, and politely inquired with sweet words and folded hands.

verses.sanskrit: परीक्षिदुवाचअहो अद्य वयं ब्रह्मन् सत्सेव्या: क्षत्रबन्धव: ।कृपयातिथिरूपेण भवद्भ‍िस्तीर्थका: कृता: ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: parīkṣid uvācaaho adya vayaṁ brahmansat-sevyāḥ kṣatra-bandhavaḥkṛpayātithi-rūpeṇabhavadbhis tīrthakāḥ kṛtāḥ
verses.text: The fortunate King Parīkṣit said: O brāhmaṇa, by your mercy only, you have sanctified us, making us like unto places of pilgrimage, all by your presence here as my guest. By your mercy, we, who are but unworthy royalty, become eligible to serve the devotee.

verses.sanskrit: येषां संस्मरणात्पुंसां सद्य: शुद्ध्यन्ति वै गृहा: ।किं पुनर्दर्शनस्पर्शपादशौचासनादिभि: ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: yeṣāṁ saṁsmaraṇāt puṁsāṁsadyaḥ śuddhyanti vai gṛhāḥkiṁ punar darśana-sparśa-pāda-śaucāsanādibhiḥ
verses.text: Simply by our remembering you, our houses become instantly sanctified. And what to speak of seeing you, touching you, washing your holy feet and offering you a seat in our home?

verses.sanskrit: सान्निध्यात्ते महायोगिन्पातकानि महान्त्यपि ।सद्यो नश्यन्ति वै पुंसां विष्णोरिव सुरेतरा: ॥ ३४ ॥
verses.transliteration: sānnidhyāt te mahā-yoginpātakāni mahānty apisadyo naśyanti vai puṁsāṁviṣṇor iva suretarāḥ
verses.text: Just as the atheist cannot remain in the presence of the Personality of Godhead, so also the invulnerable sins of a man are immediately vanquished in your presence, O saint! O great mystic!

verses.sanskrit: अपि मे भगवान् प्रीत: कृष्ण: पाण्डुसुतप्रिय: ।पैतृष्वसेयप्रीत्यर्थं तद्गोत्रस्यात्तबान्धव: ॥ ३५ ॥
verses.transliteration: api me bhagavān prītaḥkṛṣṇaḥ pāṇḍu-suta-priyaḥpaitṛ-ṣvaseya-prīty-arthaṁtad-gotrasyātta-bāndhavaḥ
verses.text: Lord Kṛṣṇa, the Personality of Godhead, who is very dear to the sons of King Pāṇḍu, has accepted me as one of those relatives just to please His great cousins and brothers.

verses.sanskrit: अन्यथा तेऽव्यक्तगतेर्दर्शनं न: कथं नृणाम् ।नितरां म्रियमाणानां संसिद्धस्य वनीयस: ॥ ३६ ॥
verses.transliteration: anyathā te ’vyakta-gaterdarśanaṁ naḥ kathaṁ nṛṇāmnitarāṁ mriyamāṇānāṁsaṁsiddhasya vanīyasaḥ
verses.text: Otherwise [without being inspired by Lord Kṛṣṇa] how is it that you have voluntarily appeared here, though you are moving incognito to the common man and are not visible to us who are on the verge of death?

verses.sanskrit: अत: पृच्छामि संसिद्धिं योगिनां परमं गुरुम् ।पुरुषस्येह यत्कार्यं म्रियमाणस्य सर्वथा ॥ ३७ ॥
verses.transliteration: ataḥ pṛcchāmi saṁsiddhiṁyogināṁ paramaṁ gurumpuruṣasyeha yat kāryaṁmriyamāṇasya sarvathā
verses.text: You are the spiritual master of great saints and devotees. I am therefore begging you to show the way of perfection for all persons, and especially for one who is about to die.

verses.sanskrit: नूनं भगवतो ब्रह्मन् गृहेषु गृहमेधिनाम् ।न लक्ष्यते ह्यवस्थानमपि गोदोहनं क्‍वचित् ॥ ३९ ॥
verses.transliteration: nūnaṁ bhagavato brahmangṛheṣu gṛha-medhināmna lakṣyate hy avasthānamapi go-dohanaṁ kvacit
verses.text: O powerful brāhmaṇa, it is said that you hardly stay in the houses of men long enough to milk a cow.
